_provenance: Structure reflects NIH SF424 (R&R) + PHS 398 Research Plan conventions. Page limits and budget caps MUST be verified against the specific NOFO and the current NIH application guide before submission.

section_intent.specific_aims: One page: long-term goal, central hypothesis, 2-3 testable aims, expected outcomes, impact.
section_intent.significance: Importance of the problem; gap in knowledge; how the project improves scientific knowledge, technical capability, or clinical practice.
section_intent.innovation: What is novel vs current paradigms; refinement/improvement/new application of concepts, methods, instrumentation, or interventions.
section_intent.approach: Per aim: rationale, design, methods, expected outcomes, potential pitfalls and alternatives, rigor of prior research, sex/biological-variable consideration.
section_intent.preliminary_data: Investigator's own data supporting feasibility and the central hypothesis (often embedded in Approach for R01).

budget_rules._modular_note: Modular budgets are requested in $25,000 modules; a per-year direct-cost ceiling applies. VERIFY current ceiling against NIH guide.

indirect._note: F&A uses the institution's federally negotiated rate; equipment and subaward portions above threshold are excluded from MTDC.

budget_rules.ai_policy: AI MUST NOT invent dollar amounts, caps, or rates. All numeric values come from the applicant. AI may only (a) check completeness, (b) flag suspected cap/category violations as 'verify against NOFO', and (c) draft justification narrative from applicant-provided numbers.

review_criteria._source: NIH peer review scored criteria (verify exact wording/weighting against current NIH review framework).
review_criteria.note: NIH has periodically revised how these are scored/grouped; verify the framework for the target council round.

reviewer_personas.focus: Scientific rigor: hypothesis testability, design, controls, statistics, feasibility of approach.
Novelty vs funded landscape and prior art; differentiation from existing R01/awards.
Completeness/compliance: required sections, page limits, budget justification, human/animal/DMS attachments.
